when did the us cut off trade with cuba
queries.generated_answer:
The US cut off trade with Cuba in varying forms. 

- Initially, a ban on all exports to Cuba was imposed on 19 October 1960.
- Later, President John F. Kennedy broadened the partial trade restrictions imposed after the revolution by Eisenhower to a ban on all trade with Cuba, except for non-subsidized sale of foods and medicines, in 1962.
